title: Get started
text: First, choose model power chatbot. ellmer shinychat support wide variety LLM providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, Vertex, Snowflake, Groq, Perplexity, . ellmer, specify LLM provider choosing corresponding chat_*() function, e.g., chat_anthropic(), chat_openai(), etc. makes easy swap chat provider different one time. Model providers also typically offer variety models. specify particular model, use chat_*() function’s model argument. example: don’t specify model argument, chat_*() function use reasonable default. information, see individual chat_*() function’s documentation.
code: ellmer::chat_openai(model = "o3")

title: Get started
text: Next, authenticate LLM provider. Popular model providers like OpenAI Anthropic require API key. recommend storing API keys .Renviron (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). can find helpful links tips getting set credentials (e.g., API key) relevant reference page chat_*() provider ’d like work (chat_openai(), chat_anthropic()).

title: Get started
text: ’ve identified model provider want use set necessary credentials, ’re ready create chatbot. following code creates basic chatbot Shiny app. Copy paste code R script, switching ellmer::chat_openai() desired chat function. Save file app.R run app. Congrats, now chat interface powered LLM choice! 🎉 Screenshot conversation using shinychat.
code: library(shiny) library(shinychat)  ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   chat_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <- ellmer::chat_openai()      observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat$stream_async(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: Let’s take closer look code app.R. shinychat chatbot includes three core steps: Create chat UI element chat_ui(). Initialize chat chat_*() function, like chat_openai(), server function. Use different chat_*() function (chat_ollama(), chat_anthropic(), etc.) use different model provider. can also use system_prompt argument supply system prompt. Set reactive listener observeEvent() waits user submit message (input$chat_user_input). message received: Send input LLM using chat$stream_async(), returns asynchronously streaming results LLM. means results appear chunks, user doesn’t wait full response. Append response chat_ui() element chat_append(), user can see model’s reply appear live ’s generated.
code: library(shiny) library(shinychat)  ui <- bslib::page_fluid(   # Add a chat UI element   chat_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Initialize a chat with your chosen model provider   chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant.")      # Listen for user input and communicate with the model   observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat$stream_async(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: Use chat_*() function’s system_prompt argument provide LLM information like behave. learn writing system prompts, see ellmer’s Prompt design vignette. Generally, recommend writing system prompt separate markdown file, prompt short can also supply directly string system_prompt argument.
code: chat <- ellmer::chat_ollama(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant")

previous_headings: Create a basic chatbot > Add messages and suggestions
title: Get started
text: can specify messages show chat first loads using chat_ui()’s messages argument. can use markdown HTML format messages. Screenshot chatbot welcome message. can also suggest inputs user adding suggestion CSS class relevant portions message. Similarly, use submit class make clicking suggestion submit input automatically. Screenshot chatbot input suggestions. markdown list (<ul> <ol>) every item contains single suggestion element automatically rendered grid clickable cards instead inline chips. suggestion accepts optional title attribute (plain text), becomes card heading; suggestion’s body becomes card description. ordered lists (<ol>), list-item number included heading. Messages can also contain arbitrary Shiny UI components, even include something like tooltip provide additional details demand.
code: chat_ui( id = "chat", messages = "**Hello!** How can I help you today?" ) messages <- ' **Hello!** How can I help you today? Here are a couple suggestions: * Tell me a joke * Tell me a story ' ui <- bslib::page_fillable( chat_ui( id = "chat", messages = messages ) )

previous_headings: Create a basic chatbot > Add messages and suggestions
title: Get started
text: can also use suggestions guide users multi-turn conversation. , ’ll need instruct AI generate suggestions adding section like one system prompt:
code: ## Showing prompt suggestions If you find it appropriate to suggest prompts the user might want to write, wrap the text of each prompt in `` tags. Also use "Suggested next steps:" to introduce the suggestions. For example: ``` Suggested next steps: 1. Suggestion 1. 2. Suggestion 2. 3. Suggestion 3. ```

title: Get started
text: Use page_fillable() fillable_mobile = TRUE want chatbot input stay anchored bottom page chat fill remaining space. Screenshot chatbot filling page.
code: ui <- bslib::page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat", messages = "Welcome!"),   fillable_mobile = TRUE )

title: Get started
text: can also useful place chat sidebar, free main panel components. Use page_sidebar() create sidebar page. , set chat sidebar’s heights 100% chat element fills sidebar. Screenshot chatbot filling sidebar.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat) ui <- bslib::page_sidebar( sidebar = sidebar( chat_ui( "chat", messages = list( "Welcome! Here is a suggestion." ), height = "100%" ), width = 300, style = "height: 100%;" ), "Main content", fillable = TRUE ) server <- function(input, output, session) { chat <- ellmer::chat_openai() observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, { stream <- chat$stream_async(input$chat_user_input) chat_append("chat", stream) }) } shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: Embedding chat component inside card() can help visually separate chat rest app. can also add card header include information chatbot (perhaps tooltip). Cards also come handy features like full_screen = TRUE make chat full-screen embedded inside larger app. Screenshot chatbot embedded card header tooltip.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  ui <- page_fillable(   card(     card_header(       "Welcome to Posit chat",       tooltip(icon("question"), "This chat is brought to you by Posit."),       class = "d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center"     ),     chat_ui(       id = "chat",       messages = "Hello! How can I help you today?"     )   ),   fillable_mobile = TRUE )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <- ellmer::chat_openai()      observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat$stream_async(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: Slash commands give users discoverable shortcuts — like /search, /clear, /help — run handler define server. Register commands object returned chat_mod_server(), using slash_command() method. user runs command, handler fires instead text sent model, happens next entirely handler. two common patterns prompt expansion — command transforms user’s input sending LLM — side effects — command performs action without involving LLM .

title: Get started
text: common use slash commands giving users shortcut sends prompt model behalf. handler takes one argument receives ContentSlashCommand object — plain string. object carries command name, text typed , text property controls LLM sees. /search shiny modules: content@command "search" content@user_text "shiny modules" content@text starts descriptive default — set expanded prompt example, /search command enrich user’s query retrieved context streaming model’s answer. real app retrieval step query vector store search index (.e., RAG workflow), core pattern : user types /search shiny modules, handler sets expanded prompt content@text streams model’s response. user sees /search shiny modules message; LLM receives expanded prompt. ContentSlashCommand extends ellmer::ContentText, works anywhere ContentText — LLM reads text property, chat UI preserves original command bookmark restore.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_mod_ui("chat", placeholder = "Type / for commands, or chat away...") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   client <- ellmer::chat_openai(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant.")   chat <- chat_mod_server("chat", client = client)    chat$slash_command("search", "Search the docs", function(content) {     # In practice, retrieve relevant documents here (e.g., via a vector DB)     content@text <- paste(       "Search the documentation for the following topic and provide a concise summary:",       content@user_text     )     stream <- client$stream(content)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: commands perform action without involving LLM — clearing conversation, opening help modal, exporting transcript. Pass echo = FALSE command doesn’t appear user message:
code: chat$slash_command("clear", "Clear the conversation", function() {   chat$clear() }, echo = FALSE)

title: Get started
text: Pass NULL handler register command appears palette handled entirely browser. Listen shiny:chat-slash-command event call preventDefault(): event cancelable bubbles. Use e.detail.id target specific chat. preventDefault() skips server round-trip; set e.detail.echo control whether command appears user message.
code: chat$slash_command("clear", "Clear the input", NULL)  tags$script(HTML("   document.addEventListener('shiny:chat-slash-command', function(e) {     if (e.detail.id !== 'chat' || e.detail.command !== 'clear') return;     e.preventDefault();     document.querySelector('#chat-chat textarea').value = '';   }); "))

title: Get started
text: Users type / open palette registered commands; arrow keys navigate, Enter Tab selects, Escape dismisses. slash command’s handler fires instead sending text model. happens next — including whether anything reaches LLM — entirely handler. / message doesn’t match registered command sent ordinary message. Handlers take 0 1 argument. 1-argument handler receives ContentSlashCommand object (ellmer::ContentText subclass) whose user_text text properties let control LLM sees preserving original command display bookmark restore. echo argument controls whether invoking command appears user message. Defaults TRUE handler. Pass echo = FALSE side-effect-handlers. slash_command() returns function removes command called. Re-registering existing name raises error unless pass force = TRUE. Slash command messages restored faithfully bookmarked app reopened. Slash commands currently available chat module (chat_mod_server()), building custom UI chat_ui() directly.

title: Get started
text: shinychat supports cancelling -progress AI response. cancellation enabled, stop button appears chat input area streaming. Users can also press Escape chat focus cancel current response. partial response already received preserved chat history.

title: Get started
text: easiest way add cancellation support use chat_mod_ui() chat_mod_server() functions. module handles everything automatically — stop button shown streaming wired internally, extra code required.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat) library(ellmer)  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_mod_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <- chat_anthropic(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant.")   chat_mod_server("chat", client = chat) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: building custom chat UI chat_ui() directly, can enable cancellation setting enable_cancel = TRUE wiring cancel input server function. key steps : Pass enable_cancel = TRUE chat_ui() show stop button streaming. Create ellmer::stream_controller() pass chat$stream_async() via controller argument. controller automatically resets streams, need create . Observe input$<id>_cancel (<id> chat element’s ID) call ctrl$cancel() fires.
code: ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat", enable_cancel = TRUE) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant.")   ctrl <- ellmer::stream_controller()    chat_task <- ExtendedTask$new(function(user_input, controller) {     stream <- chat$stream_async(       user_input,       stream = "content",       controller = controller     )     p <- promises::promise_resolve(stream)     promises::then(p, function(stream) {       chat_append("chat", stream)     })   })    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     chat_task$invoke(input$chat_user_input, controller = ctrl)   })    observeEvent(input$chat_cancel, {     ctrl$cancel()   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: shinychat supports file attachments, allowing users upload images, PDFs, text files alongside messages. attachments enabled, chat input shows file picker button also accepts drag--drop clipboard paste.

title: Get started
text: chat_mod_ui() chat_mod_server() functions enable file attachments default. Uploaded files automatically converted ellmer content objects sent model — extra code needed.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat) library(ellmer)  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_mod_ui("chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <- chat_anthropic(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant.")   chat_mod_server("chat", client = chat) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started
text: building custom chat UI chat_ui() directly, enable attachments setting allow_attachments = TRUE. changes shape input$<id>_user_input plain character string list ellmer Content objects. Use splice operator (!!!) pass content objects chat client. Key points: Pass allow_attachments = TRUE chat_ui() show file picker button. can also pass character vector MIME types (e.g. c("image/png", "image/jpeg")) restrict accepted file types. allow_attachments enabled, input$<id>_user_input always list ellmer Content objects (text first, one content object per attachment), even files attached. Use !!! splice list stream_async(). maximum combined attachment size defaults approximately 30 MB can configured via SHINYCHAT_MAX_ATTACHMENT_SIZE environment variable.
code: ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat", allow_attachments = TRUE) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant.")    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat$stream_async(!!!input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Tool Calling UI
text: shinychat automatically displays rich UI tool calls results, providing users intuitive view tools executed outcomes. tool UI works ---box ellmer requires additional configuration get started.

title: Tool Calling UI
text: Let’s start simple weather forecasting tool demonstrate default behavior: ellmer, register tool chat object. LLM calls tool, ellmer automatically evaluates tool call returns result LLM. ’re interested learning use create tools ellmer, recommend reading Tool/function calling article ellmer website. ’re working interactively R console—’ve set echo = "output" option—ellmer shows tool calls made gives preview tool result. shinychat’s tool UI works way, rich output displays shown directly chat_ui() interface Shiny apps. LLM calls tool, shinychat automatically displays tool request collapsed card. Expanding card shows arguments passed tool. tool result completed, shinychat replaces request card containing tool result: tool throws error called, ellmer captures error shows LLM. shinychat updates tool card show error message: use chat_app() chat UI module via chat_mod_ui() chat_mod_server(), shinychat automatically handles tool requests results, displaying chat interface. hand, ’re using chat_ui() calling chat_append() stream chat output, ’ll need make sure ellmer streams tool requests results shinychat setting stream = "content" $stream_async() call. Setting stream = "content" tells ellmer stream ellmer::Content objects rather plain text. result, ellmer::ContentToolRequest ellmer::ContentToolResult objects streamed shinychat, automatically displays tool requests results chat interface.
code: library(shinychat) library(ellmer) library(weathR) # for forecasts via `point_tomorrow()`  get_weather_forecast <- tool(   function(lat, lon) {     point_tomorrow(lat, lon, short = FALSE)   },   name = "get_weather_forecast",   description = "Get the weather forecast for a location.",   arguments = list(     lat = type_number("Latitude"),     lon = type_number("Longitude")   ) ) chat <- ellmer::chat("openai/gpt-4.1-nano", echo = "output") chat$register_tool(get_weather_forecast) chat$chat("What's the weather in Boston like today?") ◯ [tool call] get_weather_forecast(lat = 42.3601, lon = -71.0589) ● #> [{"time":"2025-08-05 11:00:00 EDT","temp":76,"dewpoint":17.7778,"hum…  The weather in Boston today is partly sunny with temperatures around 75-76°F during the afternoon. There is some humidity at about 64-66%. There is a possibility of rain starting around 4 PM, with increasing chances in the evening. Winds are coming from the northeast at about 8-10 mph. ◯ [tool call] get_weather_forecast(lat = 42.3601, lon = -71.0589) ● #> [{"time":"2025-08-05 11:00:00 EDT","temp":76,"dewpoint":17.7778,"hum… server <- function(input, output, session) {   client <- ellmer::chat("openai/gpt-4.1-nano")   client$register_tool(get_weather_forecast)    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- client$stream_async(input$chat_user_input, stream = "content")     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }

title: Tool Calling UI
text: can improve visual presentation adding annotations ellmer::tool() definition using ellmer::tool_annotations(). tool annotations include title icon, shinychat use tool request result displays. Now tool request shows custom title tool results show custom title icon.
code: get_weather_forecast <- tool(   function(lat, lon) {     point_tomorrow(lat, lon, short = FALSE)   },   name = "get_weather_forecast",   description = "Get the weather forecast for a location.",   arguments = list(     lat = type_number("Latitude"),     lon = type_number("Longitude")   ),   annotations = tool_annotations(     title = "Weather Forecast",     icon = bsicons::bs_icon("cloud-sun")   ) )

title: Tool Calling UI
text: Using tool annotations easy way set title icon tool requests results, sometimes ’ll want customize display specific tool calls results. cases, ’ll need update tool function return ellmer::ContentToolResult object, takes extra property includes list extra data attach result. shinychat looks uses display list within extra customize tool result shown. properties can computed dynamically based tool’s input data prepares show LLM. list contains title icon items, values override values tool’s annotations. One useful strategy include display parameters tool’s function signature, allowing LLM include context additional information tool call. example , ’ll let LLM write tool result title ’ll pick icon based forecasted temperatures. Note need also update tool’s arguments include new location_name parameter LLM can provide meaningful title. Now tool result completed, shinychat shows custom title icon based forecasted temperature:
code: get_weather_forecast <- tool(   function(lat, lon, location_name) {     forecast <- point_tomorrow(lat, lon, short = FALSE)      icon <- if (any(forecast$temp > 70)) {       bsicons::bs_icon("sun-fill")     } else if (any(forecast$temp < 45)) {       bsicons::bs_icon("snow")     } else {       bsicons::bs_icon("cloud-sun-fill")     }      ContentToolResult(       forecast,       extra = list(         display = list(           title = paste("Weather Forecast for", location_name),           icon = icon         )       )     )   },   name = "get_weather_forecast",   description = "Get the weather forecast for a location.",   arguments = list(     lat = type_number("Latitude"),     lon = type_number("Longitude"),     location_name = type_string("Name of the location for display to the user")   ),   annotations = tool_annotations(     title = "Weather Forecast",     icon = bsicons::bs_icon("cloud-sun")   ) )

title: Tool Calling UI
text: last example, saw include arguments tool function let LLM write display text us (e.g., location name). strategy useful shinychat automatically supports `_intent` argument. `_intent` present tool’s arguments, shinychat shows value tool request result titles reason tool called. demonstrate, ’ll use take break weather tool create tool simply generates random number. , tool doesn’t need arguments, include `_intent` argument tool function include description explain purpose. tool called, shinychat shows reason LLM called tool header tool card.
code: tool_random_number <- tool(   function(`_intent`) {     runif(1)   },   name = "tool_random_number",   description = "Generate a random number.",   arguments = list(     `_intent` = type_string(       "A short snippet used for display purposes to explain the call to the user."     )   ),   annotations = tool_annotations(     title = "Random Number Generator",     icon = bsicons::bs_icon("dice-3-fill")   ) )

title: Tool Calling UI
text: ’ve already seen can customize tool title icon returning ContentToolResult object tool function specifying title icon extra$display list. shinychat uses display list two additional categories customization: Alternative html, markdown, text show user place text value shown LLM. Options control tool result presented.

title: Tool Calling UI
text: default, shinychat shows tool result’s value property code block user. often sufficient, cases tool may collect prepare data better presented users different format. example, weather tool returns data frame forecast next day. LLM sees JSON representation data frame, users likely prefer see nicely formatted table. shinychat looks following properties display list shows user first available display following order: html: HTML string object. can HTML generated via R packages like htmltools, gt, reactable even htmlwidgets. markdown: markdown string automatically rendered rich HTML shinychat. text: plain text string shown without code formatting. Keep mind content show users faithfully represent value shown LLM. weather tool great example—JSON object isn’t user-friendly, table showing forecast data perfect.

previous_headings: Customizing tool result display > Alternative display content
title: Tool Calling UI
text: following example, ’ll update get_weather_forecast() show user nicely formatted HTML table using gt. Expand tool result card see forecast table. Notice fullscreen toggle button card body—clicking expands card fill viewport.
code: get_weather_forecast <- tool(   function(lat, lon, location_name) {     forecast_data <- point_tomorrow(lat, lon, short = FALSE)     forecast_table <- gt::as_raw_html(gt::gt(forecast_data))      ContentToolResult(       forecast_data,       extra = list(         display = list(           html = forecast_table,           title = paste("Weather Forecast for", location_name),           full_screen = TRUE         )       )     )   },   name = "get_weather_forecast",   description = "Get the weather forecast for a location.",   arguments = list(     lat = type_number("Latitude"),     lon = type_number("Longitude"),     location_name = type_string("Name of the location for display to the user")   ),   annotations = tool_annotations(     title = "Weather Forecast",     icon = bsicons::bs_icon("cloud-sun")   ) )

previous_headings: Customizing tool result display > Alternative display content
title: Tool Calling UI
text: can also prepare markdown content show users based tool result. ’s less appropriate weather tool, use markdown summarize forecast sentences.
code: get_weather_forecast <- tool(   function(lat, lon, location_name) {     forecast_data <- point_tomorrow(lat, lon, short = FALSE)      temp_current <- forecast_data$temp[1]     skies_current <- forecast_data$skies[[1]]      temp_high <- max(forecast_data$temp)     temp_low <- min(forecast_data$temp)      humidity <- round(mean(forecast_data$humidity), 1)     skies <- table(forecast_data$skies)     skies <- names(skies)[which.max(skies)]      forecast_summary <- glue::glue(       "In **{location_name}**, it's currently {temp_current}°F with _{tolower(skies_current)}_ skies. ",       "Today's high will be {temp_high}°F and the low will be {temp_low}°F. ",       "Humidity is around {humidity}%. ",       "Look for **{tolower(skies)}** skies throughout the day."     )      ContentToolResult(       forecast_data,       extra = list(         display = list(           markdown = forecast_summary,           title = paste("Weather Forecast for", location_name)         )       )     )   },   name = "get_weather_forecast",   description = "Get the weather forecast for a location.",   arguments = list(     lat = type_number("Latitude"),     lon = type_number("Longitude"),     location_name = type_string("Name of the location for display to the user")   ),   annotations = tool_annotations(     title = "Weather Forecast",     icon = bsicons::bs_icon("cloud-sun")   ) )

title: Tool Calling UI
text: addition customizing tool title icon providing alternative display content, can also control tool result presented using options display list. shinychat supports several options display list control results presented: show_request = FALSE: Hide tool call details tool results display. useful rich output tool call details clear rest display. example, might appropriate hide request details showing full formatted table results. open = TRUE: Expand result panel default. useful ’ve customized tool display include {htmlwidget} rich content users see immediately. full_screen = TRUE: Add fullscreen toggle button tool result card. clicked, card expands fill entire viewport, making easy inspect large detailed content like maps, tables, plots. Users can exit fullscreen pressing Escape, clicking backdrop, using close button. title icon: Choose title icon displayed tool result card header.

title: Tool Calling UI
text: shinychat uses rich tool UI described default, can choose hide tool calls use shinychat’s basic display without allowing rich alternative content displays. users won’t need customize options, can useful use basic display debugging verification purposes need see tool arguments outputs exactly written seen LLM. adjust display debugging verification purposes, set shinychat.tool_display option (SHINYCHAT_TOOL_DISPLAY environment variable) one following values: users, default "rich" display provides best experience.
code: # Disable tool UI entirely options(shinychat.tool_display = "none")  # Use basic text-based display (useful for verifying inputs/outputs) options(shinychat.tool_display = "basic")  # Default rich display applies customizations from tool authors options(shinychat.tool_display = "rich")

title: Chat UI Component for shiny
text: shinychat provides Shiny toolkit building generative AI applications like chatbots streaming content. ’s designed work alongside ellmer package, handles response generation.

title: Chat UI Component for shiny
text: can install shinychat CRAN : , install development version shinychat GitHub :
code: install.packages("shinychat") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("posit-dev/shinychat/pkg-r")

title: Chat UI Component for shiny
text: run example, ’ll first need create OpenAI API key, set environment OPENAI_API_KEY. ’ll also need install ellmer package (install.packages("ellmer")).
code: library(shiny) library(shinychat)  ui <- bslib::page_fillable(   chat_ui(     id = "chat",     messages = "**Hello!** How can I help you today?"   ),   fillable_mobile = TRUE )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat <-     ellmer::chat_openai(       system_prompt = "Respond to the user as succinctly as possible."     )    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat$stream_async(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Chat UI Component for shiny
text: Ready start building chatbot shinychat? See Get Started learn .

title: Slash command content — ContentSlashCommand
text: ellmer::ContentText subclass preserves original slash command entered user. chat UI restored bookmark (pre-existing turns), original /command user_text shown UI instead (possibly transformed) text sent LLM.

title: Slash command content — ContentSlashCommand
code: ContentSlashCommand(   text = stop("Required"),   command = character(0),   user_text = "" )

title: Slash command content — ContentSlashCommand
text: text text sent LLM. constructed chat module, defaults descriptive string like "user entered /greet slash command arguments: world". Set handler control LLM actually sees. command slash command name (without leading /). user_text text user typed command name. Defaults "".

title: Slash command content — ContentSlashCommand
text: ContentSlashCommand object.

title: Slash command content — ContentSlashCommand
text: Slash command handlers accept argument receive ContentSlashCommand object rather plain string. object three properties: command: command name (e.g., "greet"). user_text: text user typed command (e.g., "world"). text: text sent LLM. starts descriptive string like "user entered /greet slash command arguments: world". Set whatever text LLM actually see. ContentSlashCommand extends ellmer::ContentText, works anywhere ContentText – including client$stream(), client$chat(), etc. LLM providers read text property (inherited behavior), contents_shinychat() reconstructs original /command user_text display chat UI. Bookmark serialization via ellmer::contents_record() / ellmer::contents_replay() automatic.

title: Slash command content — ContentSlashCommand
text: LLM sees "Say hello world", restore chat UI shows /greet world.
code: chat$slash_command("greet", "Greet someone", function(content) {   content@text <- paste("Say hello to", content@user_text)   stream <- client$stream(content)   chat_append("chat", stream) })

what: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
text: Create simple Shiny app live chatting using ellmer::Chat object. Note functions mutate input client object chat turns appended history. app created chat_app() suitable interactive use single user. multi-user Shiny apps, use Shiny module chat functions – chat_mod_ui() chat_mod_server() – sure create new chat client user session.

title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
code: chat_app(client, ..., bookmark_store = "url", allow_attachments = TRUE)  chat_mod_ui(   id,   ...,   client = deprecated(),   messages = NULL,   allow_attachments = TRUE )  chat_mod_server(   id,   client,   greeting = NULL,   bookmark_on_input = TRUE,   bookmark_on_response = TRUE )

title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
text: client chat object created ellmer, e.g. ellmer::chat_openai() friends. argument deprecated chat_mod_ui() client state now managed chat_mod_server(). ... chat_app(), additional arguments passed shiny::shinyApp(). chat_mod_ui(), additional arguments passed chat_ui(). bookmark_store bookmarking store use app. Passed enable_bookmarking shiny::shinyApp(). Defaults "url", uses URL store chat state. URL-based bookmarking limited size; use "server" store state server side without size limitations; disable bookmarking setting "disable". allow_attachments Controls file-attachment affordance (attach button, plus clipboard paste drag--drop) chat input. Pass TRUE accept supported types (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, PDF, common text/code files Markdown, plain text, CSV, JSON, source files), FALSE disable, character vector MIME types restrict accepted (must one supported types). shape input$<id>_user_input determined argument, predictable given app. attachments disabled (default), typed text character string, exactly . attachments enabled, always list ellmer ellmer::Content objects (typed text, , followed one content object per attachment) - list even files attached. Splice list chat method's ... !!!, e.g. client$stream_async(!!!input$<id>_user_input). (rlang::inject() needed: ellmer's chat methods collect ... dynamic dots.) maximum combined size attachments single message controlled globally SHINYCHAT_MAX_ATTACHMENT_SIZE environment variable (raw byte count; defaults approximately 30 MB). Files push total cap rejected browser notice. id chat module ID. messages Initial messages shown chat, used client (chat_mod_ui()) already contain turns. Passed messages chat_ui(). greeting Optional greeting set module initializes. Accepts static value (string, htmltools::HTML(), htmltools::tagList(), chat_greeting()) function generates greeting dynamically. See Greeting section details. bookmark_on_input logical value determines bookmark updated user submits message. Default TRUE. bookmark_on_response logical value determines bookmark updated response stream completes. Default TRUE.

title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
text: chat_app() returns shiny::shinyApp() object. chat_mod_ui() returns UI shinychat module. chat_mod_server() includes shinychat module server logic, returns environment containing: last_input: reactive value containing last user input (string attachments disabled, list ellmer Content objects enabled). last_turn: reactive value containing last assistant turn. update_user_input(): function update chat input submit new user input. Takes arguments update_chat_user_input(), except id session, supplied automatically. append(): function append new message chat UI. Takes arguments chat_append(), except id session, supplied automatically. clear(): function clear chat history chat UI. clear() takes optional list messages used initialize chat clearing. messages list messages, message list role content fields. client_history argument controls chat client's history updated clearing. can one : "clear" chat history; "set" chat history messages; "append" messages existing chat history; "keep" existing chat history. set_greeting(): function set, stream, clear chat greeting. Pass chat_greeting() object, plain string, NULL clear. Streaming greetings run inside shiny::ExtendedTask session stays responsive; called greeting already streaming, new greeting queued. greeting already dismissed, calling set_greeting() updates content make visible ; call clear(greeting = TRUE) first show new greeting dismissal. status: reactive value indicating current chat interaction state. Returns "idle" response progress, "streaming" response actively received. client: current chat client object (active binding always reflects latest client, even set_client() called). set_client(new_client, sync = TRUE): Replace chat client used module. sync TRUE (default), new client inherits conversation turns, system prompt, tools previous client conversation continues seamlessly. Set sync = FALSE use new client -. response currently streaming, swap deferred stream completes. called multiple times streaming, recent new client used. slash_command(name, description, handler, ..., echo, force): Register slash command. handler required: pass function (taking 0 1 argument), NULL client-side command handled JavaScript via shiny:chat-slash-command DOM event. handler takes one argument receives ContentSlashCommand object (plain string). See ContentSlashCommand details use object preserve original command text across bookmarks. echo controls whether invoking command echoed user message awaits response; defaults TRUE handler given FALSE otherwise (set echo = FALSE handler performs side effects). Returns function removes command. Errors command name already registered unless force = TRUE.

title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
text: chat_app(): simple Shiny app live chatting. Note app suitable interactive use single user; use chat_app() multi-user Shiny app context. chat_mod_ui(): simple chat app module UI. chat_mod_server(): simple chat app module server.

title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
text: greeting function, module calls time greeting_requested event fires — first view chat empty, clear(greeting = TRUE). function return chat_greeting() (typically wrapping stream). Static values (strings, chat_greeting() objects) set init regenerate. module detects named arguments greeting function decide pass. Currently recognized argument client. function(client) (recommended). module clones client passed chat_mod_server(), wipes turn history, passes fresh clone client. avoids manually creating configuring separate client:   function() (zero arguments). create manage client:   Static value. Set ; regenerate clear():   returned set_greeting() helper available cases need set greeting outside greeting lifecycle.
code: chat_mod_server("chat", client, greeting = function(client) {   stream <- client$stream_async("Generate a short welcome message.")   chat_greeting(stream) }) chat_mod_server("chat", client, greeting = function() {   greeter <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o")   stream <- greeter$stream_async("Generate a short welcome message.")   chat_greeting(stream) }) chat_mod_server("chat", client, greeting = "## Welcome!\n\nHow can I help?")

title: Open a live chat application in the browser — chat_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Interactive in the console ---- client <- ellmer::chat_anthropic() chat_app(client)  # Inside a Shiny app ---- library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  ui <- page_fillable(   titlePanel("shinychat example"),    layout_columns(     card(       card_header("Chat with Claude"),       chat_mod_ui(         "claude",         messages = list(           "Hi! Use this chat interface to chat with Anthropic's `claude-3-5-sonnet`."         )       )     ),     card(       card_header("Chat with ChatGPT"),       chat_mod_ui(         "openai",         messages = list(           "Hi! Use this chat interface to chat with OpenAI's `gpt-4o`."         )       )     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   claude <- ellmer::chat_anthropic(model = "claude-3-5-sonnet-latest") # Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY   openai <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o") # Requires OPENAI_API_KEY    chat_mod_server("claude", claude)   chat_mod_server("openai", openai) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } # }

what: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
title: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
text: chat_append function appends message existing chat_ui(). response can string, string generator, string promise, string promise generator (returned 'ellmer' package's chat, stream, chat_async, stream_async methods, respectively). function called Shiny app's server. generally used append client's response chat, user messages added chat UI automatically front-end. need use chat_append(role="user") programmatically generating queries server sending behalf user, want reflected UI.

title: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
code: chat_append(   id,   response,   role = c("assistant", "user"),   icon = NULL,   session = getDefaultReactiveDomain() )

title: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
text: id ID chat element response message message stream append chat element. actual message content can one following: string, interpreted markdown rendered HTML client. prevent interpreting markdown, mark string htmltools::HTML(). UI element. includes htmltools::tagList(), take UI elements (including strings) children. case, strings still interpreted markdown long inside HTML. role role message (either "assistant" "user"). Defaults "assistant". icon optional icon display next message, currently used assistant messages. icon can HTML element (e.g., htmltools::img() tag) string HTML. session Shiny session object

title: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
text: Returns promise resolves contents stream, error. promise resolves message successfully sent client; note guarantee message actually received rendered client. promise rejects error occurs processing response (see "Error handling" section).

title: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
text: response argument generator, promise, promise generator, error occurs producing message (e.g., iteration stream_async fails), promise returned chat_append reject error. chat_append call last expression Shiny observer, shinychat log error message show message error occurred chat UI.

title: Append an assistant response (or user message) to a chat control — chat_append
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(coro) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # Dumbest chatbot in the world: ignores user input and chooses # a random, vague response. fake_chatbot <- async_generator(function(input) {   responses <- c(     "What does that suggest to you?",     "I see.",     "I'm not sure I understand you fully.",     "What do you think?",     "Can you elaborate on that?",     "Interesting question! Let's examine thi... **See more**"   )    await(async_sleep(1))   for (chunk in strsplit(sample(responses, 1), "")[[1]]) {     yield(chunk)     await(async_sleep(0.02))   } })  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat", fill = TRUE) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     response <- fake_chatbot(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", response)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Low-level function to append a message to a chat control — chat_append_message
title: Low-level function to append a message to a chat control — chat_append_message
text: advanced users want control message chunking behavior. users use chat_append() instead.

title: Low-level function to append a message to a chat control — chat_append_message
code: chat_append_message(   id,   msg,   chunk = TRUE,   operation = c("append", "replace"),   icon = NULL,   session = getDefaultReactiveDomain() )

title: Low-level function to append a message to a chat control — chat_append_message
text: id ID chat element msg message append. named list role content fields. role field either "user" "assistant". content field string containing message content, Markdown format. chunk Whether msg just chunk message, , type. FALSE, msg complete message. "start", msg first chunk multi-chunk message. "end", msg last chunk multi-chunk message. TRUE, msg intermediate chunk multi-chunk message. Default FALSE. operation operation perform message. "append", new content appended existing message content. "replace", existing message content replaced new content. Ignored chunk FALSE. icon optional icon display next message, currently used assistant messages. icon can HTML element (e.g., htmltools::img() tag) string HTML. session Shiny session object

title: Low-level function to append a message to a chat control — chat_append_message
text: Returns nothing (invisible(NULL)).

title: Low-level function to append a message to a chat control — chat_append_message
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(coro) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # Dumbest chatbot in the world: ignores user input and chooses # a random, vague response. fake_chatbot <- async_generator(function(id, input) {   responses <- c(     "What does that suggest to you?",     "I see.",     "I'm not sure I understand you fully.",     "What do you think?",     "Can you elaborate on that?",     "Interesting question! Let's examine thi... **See more**"   )    # Use low-level chat_append_message() to temporarily set a progress message   chat_append_message(id, list(role = "assistant", content = "_Thinking..._ "))   await(async_sleep(1))   # Clear the progress message   chat_append_message(id, list(role = "assistant", content = ""), operation = "replace")    for (chunk in strsplit(sample(responses, 1), "")[[1]]) {     yield(chunk)     await(async_sleep(0.02))   } })  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat", fill = TRUE) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     response <- fake_chatbot("chat", input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", response)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Create an attachment from a local file path — chat_attachment
title: Create an attachment from a local file path — chat_attachment
text: Reads file, base64-encodes contents, returns list format expected attachments argument update_chat_user_input().

title: Create an attachment from a local file path — chat_attachment
code: chat_attachment(path, mime = NULL, name = NULL)

title: Create an attachment from a local file path — chat_attachment
text: path Path file. file must exist. mime MIME type file. NULL (default), guessed file extension. Raises error extension unrecognised. name Filename shown attachment chip. Defaults basename(path).

title: Create an attachment from a local file path — chat_attachment
text: list elements mime, name, size, data_url, ready pass element attachments argument update_chat_user_input().

what: Clear all messages from a chat control — chat_clear
title: Clear all messages from a chat control — chat_clear
text: Removes messages chat UI. Set greeting = TRUE also clear greeting, re-triggers greeting_requested (see Greeting section chat_ui()).

title: Clear all messages from a chat control — chat_clear
code: chat_clear(id, greeting = FALSE, session = getDefaultReactiveDomain())

title: Clear all messages from a chat control — chat_clear
text: id ID chat element greeting TRUE, also clears greeting. greeting cleared, greeting_requested fire (chat visible), allowing server generate new greeting. session Shiny session object

title: Clear all messages from a chat control — chat_clear
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(shiny) library(bslib)  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat", fill = TRUE),   actionButton("clear", "Clear chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$clear, {     chat_clear("chat")   })    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     response <- paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", response)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # Regenerate greeting on clear ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat"),   actionButton("new_chat", "New chat") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$chat_greeting_requested, {     chat_set_greeting("chat", "## Welcome!\n\nHow can I help?")   })    observeEvent(input$new_chat, {     # Clearing with greeting = TRUE triggers greeting_requested again     chat_clear("chat", greeting = TRUE)   })    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     chat_append("chat", paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

code: chat_greeting(content, dismissible = TRUE)

text: content greeting content. Can : string, interpreted markdown. htmltools::HTML() object, rendered raw HTML. htmltools tag htmltools::tagList(), including Shiny inputs/outputs. generator promise (valid used chat_set_greeting()). dismissible Whether greeting automatically dismissed user sends message. Defaults TRUE.

title: Create a greeting for a chat UI — chat_greeting
text: S3 object class "chat_greeting".

title: Create a greeting for a chat UI — chat_greeting
text: Non-dismissible greeting (stays visible user sends message):   Greeting suggestion cards (clickable chips fill input):   Greeting HTML tags (Shiny inputs/outputs):
code: chat_greeting("Please read our [terms of service](https://example.com).", dismissible = FALSE) chat_greeting(paste( "## Welcome! ", "Try one of these: ", 'Summarize my data ', 'Create a plot ', 'Explain this code' )) chat_greeting(htmltools::tagList( htmltools::h2("Welcome!"), shiny::selectInput("model", "Choose a model:", c("gpt-4o", "claude-3")) ))

title: Create a greeting for a chat UI — chat_greeting
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui(     "chat",     greeting = chat_greeting("## Welcome!\n\nHow can I help you today?")   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     response <- paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", response)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Add Shiny bookmarking for shinychat — chat_restore
title: Add Shiny bookmarking for shinychat — chat_restore
text: Adds Shiny bookmarking hooks save restore ellmer chat client. Also restores chat messages history client. either bookmark_on_input bookmark_on_response TRUE, Shiny App's bookmark automatically updated without showing modal user. Note: client's chat state saved/restored bookmark. client's state properly capture chat's UI (.e., transformation applied -receiving displaying message), may need implement session$onRestore() (possibly session$onBookmark) handler restore additional state. avoid restoring chat history client, can ensure history empty calling client$set_turns(list()) passing client chat_restore().

title: Add Shiny bookmarking for shinychat — chat_restore
code: chat_restore(   id,   client,   ...,   bookmark_on_input = TRUE,   bookmark_on_response = TRUE,   restore_ui = TRUE,   session = getDefaultReactiveDomain() )

title: Add Shiny bookmarking for shinychat — chat_restore
text: id ID chat element client ellmer LLM chat client. ... Used future parameter expansion. bookmark_on_input logical value determines bookmark updated user submits message. Default TRUE. bookmark_on_response logical value determines bookmark updated response stream completes. Default TRUE. restore_ui Whether render client's existing turns chat UI registration. Default TRUE. Set FALSE re-registering bookmarks client swap (UI already reflects conversation). session Shiny session object

title: Add Shiny bookmarking for shinychat — chat_restore
text: Invisibly returns function , called, cancels bookmark registrations made call. useful swapping chat client: cancel previous bookmarks, call chat_restore() new client.

title: Add Shiny bookmarking for shinychat — chat_restore
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  ui <- function(request) {   page_fillable(     chat_ui("chat", fill = TRUE)   ) }  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat_client <- ellmer::chat_ollama(     system_prompt = "Important: Always respond in a limerick",     model = "qwen2.5-coder:1.5b",     echo = TRUE   )   # Update bookmark to chat on user submission and completed response   chat_restore("chat", chat_client)    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat_client$stream_async(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  # Enable bookmarking! shinyApp(ui, server, enableBookmarking = "server") }

what: Set the greeting for a chat UI — chat_set_greeting
title: Set the greeting for a chat UI — chat_set_greeting
text: Sets clears greeting displayed existing chat_ui(). greeting shown chat first loads dismissed user sends first message. Call chat_set_greeting() server display dynamic streaming greeting. typically used inside shiny::observeEvent() watching input$<id>_greeting_requested – event fires chat visible, messages, greeting set. See Greeting section chat_ui() details greeting_requested. greeting already dismissed, calling function updates greeting content make visible . show new greeting dismissal, first clear chat chat_clear(id, greeting = TRUE). Pass NULL clear current greeting entirely.

title: Set the greeting for a chat UI — chat_set_greeting
code: chat_set_greeting(id, greeting, session = getDefaultReactiveDomain())

title: Set the greeting for a chat UI — chat_set_greeting
text: id ID chat element greeting greeting display. Can : NULL clear current greeting. plain string htmltools::HTML() object (auto-wrapped default options). chat_greeting() object. session Shiny session object

title: Set the greeting for a chat UI — chat_set_greeting
text: Returns invisible(NULL) static NULL greetings, promise streaming greetings (resolves streaming complete).

title: Set the greeting for a chat UI — chat_set_greeting
code:
if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # Static greeting set from the server ui <- page_fillable(chat_ui("chat"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat_set_greeting("chat", "## Welcome!

How can I help you today?")    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     chat_append("chat", paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat) library(coro)  # Streaming greeting generated by a function greeting_generator <- async_generator(function() {   for (chunk in strsplit("## Hello!

How can I help you?", "")[[1]]) {     yield(chunk)     await(async_sleep(0.02))   } })  ui <- page_fillable(chat_ui("chat"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat_set_greeting("chat", chat_greeting(greeting_generator()))    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     chat_append("chat", paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # LLM-generated greeting using greeting_requested ui <- page_fillable(chat_ui("chat"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   chat_client <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-4o")    observeEvent(input$chat_greeting_requested, {     stream <- chat_client$stream_async(       "Generate a short, friendly welcome message."     )     chat_set_greeting("chat", chat_greeting(stream))   })    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     stream <- chat_client$stream_async(input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", stream)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # Regenerate pattern: chat_clear(greeting = TRUE) triggers greeting_requested ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat"),   actionButton("regenerate", "New greeting") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$chat_greeting_requested, {     chat_set_greeting(       "chat",       paste("## Welcome!

Generated at", Sys.time())     )   })    observeEvent(input$regenerate, {     chat_clear("chat", greeting = TRUE)   })    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     chat_append("chat", paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input))   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

Extra HTML attributes include chat element messages list messages prepopulate chat . message can one following: string, interpreted markdown rendered HTML client. prevent interpreting markdown, mark string htmltools::HTML(). Can HTML tag form htmltools::HTML() htmltools::tags(). None, default robot icon used. enable_cancel TRUE, show stop button streaming allows user cancel -progress response. using chat_mod_server(), cancellation wired automatically. manual usage chat_ui(), observe input$<id>_cancel handle cancellation (e.g., calling ctrl$cancel() ellmer stream_controller()). "enter+modifier": Ctrl+Enter (Cmd+Enter Mac) submits, plain Enter adds newline. allow_attachments Controls file-attachment affordance (attach button, plus clipboard paste drag--drop) chat input. Pass TRUE accept supported types (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, PDF, common text/code files Markdown, plain text, CSV, JSON, source files), FALSE disable, character vector MIME types restrict accepted (must one supported types). shape input$<id>_user_input determined argument, predictable given app. attachments disabled (default), typed text character string, exactly . attachments enabled, always list ellmer ellmer::Content objects (typed text, , followed one content object per attachment) - list even files attached. Splice list chat method's ... !!!, e.g. client$stream_async(!!!input$<id>_user_input). (rlang::inject() needed: ellmer's chat methods collect ... dynamic dots.) maximum combined size attachments single message controlled globally SHINYCHAT_MAX_ATTACHMENT_SIZE environment variable (raw byte count; defaults approximately 30 MB). Files push total cap rejected browser notice. footer Optional HTML content display chat input. can HTML content (tags, tag lists, character strings). title: Create a chat UI element — chat_ui
text: model produces reasoning "thinking" tokens, shinychat renders collapsible panel response. panel shows live stream model's reasoning thinks, auto-collapses response begins. Thinking display works automatically model supports . Two paths supported: ellmer's ContentThinking objects. Models provide structured thinking API (e.g., Claude extended thinking) emit ContentThinking objects stream stream = "content". shinychat detects routes thinking panel. chat_append() uses internally pass ellmer content stream. Raw <thinking> tags. Many open-source local models (DeepSeek, QwQ, Qwen, etc.) emit <thinking>...</thinking> tags directly markdown output. shinychat detects tags streaming renders enclosed text thinking panel extra configuration.

title: Create a chat UI element — chat_ui
text: can optionally get labeled sub-sections within thinking panel asking model emit <topic>...</topic> tags reasoning. extracted rendered section headings inside thinking display, current topic appears collapsed header live status. use topic labels, add something like system prompt:   Topic labels entirely optional. Without , thinking panel still works – just sub-section headings.
code: When thinking through a problem, wrap brief topic labels in <topic> tags to indicate what you're currently reasoning about. For example: <topic>parsing the input</topic>

title: Create a chat UI element — chat_ui
code:
if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat) ui <- page_fillable( chat_ui("chat", fill = TRUE) ) server <- function(input, output, session) { observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, { # In a real app, this would call out to a chat client or API, # perhaps using the 'ellmer' package. response <- paste0( "You said:\n\n", "
", htmltools::htmlEscape(input$chat_user_input), "
" ) chat_append("chat", response) chat_append("chat", stream) }) } shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Format ellmer content for shinychat — contents_shinychat
title: Format ellmer content for shinychat — contents_shinychat
text: Format ellmer content shinychat

title: Format ellmer content for shinychat — contents_shinychat
code: contents_shinychat(content)

title: Format ellmer content for shinychat — contents_shinychat
text: content ellmer::Content object.

title: Format ellmer content for shinychat — contents_shinychat
text: Returns text, HTML, web component tags formatted use chat_ui().

title: Format ellmer content for shinychat — contents_shinychat
text: can extend contents_shinychat() handle custom content types application. contents_shinychat() S7 generic. worked S7 , can learn S7 classes, generics methods S7 documentation. work short example creating custom display results tool gets local weather forecasts. first need create custom class extends ellmer::ContentToolResult.   Next, create simple ellmer::tool() gets weather forecast location returns custom WeatherToolResult class. custom class works just like regular ContentToolResult, additional location_name property.   Finally, can extend contents_shinychat() render custom content class display chat interface. basic process define contents_shinychat() external generic implement method custom class.   can use pattern completely customize content displayed inside shinychat returning HTML objects directly method. can also use pattern build upon default shinychat display tool requests results. using S7::super(), can create object shinychat uses tool results (tool requests), modify suit needs.   Note need create new class extend contents_shinychat() customize tool display. Rather, can use strategies discussed Tool Calling UI article customize tool request result display providing display list extra argument tool result.
code: library(ellmer)  WeatherToolResult <- S7::new_class(   "WeatherToolResult",   parent = ContentToolResult,   properties = list(     location_name = S7::class_character   ) ) get_weather_forecast <- tool(   function(lat, lon, location_name) {     WeatherToolResult(       weathR::point_tomorrow(lat, lon, short = FALSE),       location_name = location_name     )   },   name = "get_weather_forecast",   description = "Get the weather forecast for a location.",   arguments = list(     lat = type_number("Latitude"),     lon = type_number("Longitude"),     location_name = type_string("Name of the location for display to the user")   ) ) contents_shinychat <- S7::new_external_generic(   package = "shinychat",   name = "contents_shinychat",   dispatch_args = "contents" )  S7::method(contents_shinychat, WeatherToolResult) <- function(content) {   # Your custom rendering logic here } S7::method(contents_shinychat, WeatherToolResult) <- function(content) {   # Call the super method for ContentToolResult to get shinychat's defaults   res <- contents_shinychat(S7::super(content, ContentToolResult))    # Then update the result object with more specific content   # In this case, we render the tool result dataframe as a {gt} table...   res$value <- gt::as_raw_html(gt::gt(content@value))   res$value_type <- "html"   # ...and update the tool result title to include the location name   res$title <- paste("Weather Forecast for", content@location_name)    res }

title: Stream markdown content — markdown_stream
text: Streams markdown content output_markdown_stream() UI element.  markdown stream can useful displaying generative AI responses (outside chat interface), streaming logs, use cases chunks content generated time.

title: Stream markdown content — markdown_stream
code: markdown_stream(   id,   content_stream,   operation = c("replace", "append"),   session = getDefaultReactiveDomain() )

title: Stream markdown content — markdown_stream
text: id ID markdown stream stream content . content_stream string generator (e.g., coro::generator() coro::async_generator()), string promise (e.g., promises::promise()), string promise generator. operation operation perform markdown stream. default, "replace", replace current content new content stream. option, "append", append new content stream existing content. session Shiny session object.

title: Stream markdown content — markdown_stream
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(shiny) library(coro) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  # Define a generator that yields a random response # (imagine this is a more sophisticated AI generator) random_response_generator <- async_generator(function() {   responses <- c(     "What does that suggest to you?",     "I see.",     "I'm not sure I understand you fully.",     "What do you think?",     "Can you elaborate on that?",     "Interesting question! Let's examine thi... **See more**"   )    await(async_sleep(1))   for (chunk in strsplit(sample(responses, 1), "")[[1]]) {     yield(chunk)     await(async_sleep(0.02))   } })  ui <- page_fillable(   actionButton("generate", "Generate response"),   output_markdown_stream("stream") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$generate, {     markdown_stream("stream", random_response_generator())   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Create a UI element for a markdown stream. — output_markdown_stream
code: output_markdown_stream(   id,   ...,   content = "",   content_type = "markdown",   auto_scroll = TRUE,   width = "min(680px, 100%)",   height = "auto" )

title: Create a UI element for a markdown stream. — output_markdown_stream
text: id unique identifier markdown stream. ... Extra HTML attributes include chat element content string content display streaming occurs. content_type Markdown HTML, may also UI element(s) input output bindings. content_type content type. Default "markdown" (specifically, CommonMark). Supported content types include: * "markdown": markdown text, specifically CommonMark * "html": rendering HTML content. * "text": plain text. auto_scroll Whether automatically scroll bottom scrollable container new content added. Default True. width width UI element. height height UI element.

title: Create a UI element for a markdown stream. — output_markdown_stream
text: shiny tag object.

what: shinychat: Chat UI Component for 'shiny' — shinychat-package
title: shinychat: Chat UI Component for 'shiny' — shinychat-package
text: Provides scrolling chat interface multiline input, suitable creating chatbot apps based Large Language Models (LLMs). Designed work particularly well 'ellmer' R package calling LLMs.

title: shinychat: Chat UI Component for 'shiny' — shinychat-package
text: Maintainer: Garrick Aden-Buie garrick@adenbuie.com (ORCID) Authors: Garrick Aden-Buie garrick@adenbuie.com (ORCID) Joe Cheng joe@posit.co Carson Sievert carson@posit.co Barret Schloerke barret@posit.co (ORCID) contributors: Posit Software, PBC (ROR) [copyright holder, funder]

what: Update the user input of a chat control — update_chat_user_input
title: Update the user input of a chat control — update_chat_user_input
text: Update user input chat control

title: Update the user input of a chat control — update_chat_user_input
code: update_chat_user_input(   id,   ...,   value = NULL,   placeholder = NULL,   submit = FALSE,   focus = FALSE,   attachments = NULL,   attachment_mode = c("append", "set"),   session = getDefaultReactiveDomain() )

title: Update the user input of a chat control — update_chat_user_input
text: id ID chat element ... Currently unused, reserved future use. value value set user input . NULL, input updated. placeholder placeholder text user input submit Whether automatically submit text user. Requires value. focus Whether move focus input element. Requires value non-empty attachments. attachments list attachment objects created chat_attachment(). NULL (default), existing staged attachments left unchanged. Pass empty list (list()) clear staged attachments. attachment_mode combine attachments already-staged attachments. "append" (default) adds existing set; "set" replaces . Use attachment_mode = "set" attachments = list() clear staged attachments. session Shiny session object

title: Update the user input of a chat control — update_chat_user_input
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() library(shiny) library(bslib) library(shinychat)  ui <- page_fillable(   chat_ui("chat"),   layout_columns(     fill = FALSE,     actionButton("update_placeholder", "Update placeholder"),     actionButton("update_value", "Update user input")   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$update_placeholder, {     update_chat_user_input("chat", placeholder = "New placeholder text")   })    observeEvent(input$update_value, {     update_chat_user_input("chat", value = "New user input", focus = TRUE)   })    observeEvent(input$chat_user_input, {     response <- paste0("You said: ", input$chat_user_input)     chat_append("chat", response)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: shinychat (development version)
text: Added file attachment support: users can upload images, PDFs, text files alongside chat messages via file picker button, drag--drop, clipboard paste. chat_mod_ui()/chat_mod_server() enable attachments default automatically convert uploads ellmer Content objects model. chat_ui(), enable allow_attachments = TRUE (MIME allow-list) splice input$<id>_user_input chat methods !!!. maximum combined attachment size defaults approximately 30 MB can configured via SHINYCHAT_MAX_ATTACHMENT_SIZE environment variable. Added slash commands: typeahead command palette lets users trigger named shortcuts directly chat input. Type / open palette, filter typing, pick command arrow keys click. Commands can expand LLM prompts, trigger server-side side effects (clear chat, open modal, export transcript), handled entirely client-side via cancelable shiny:chat-slash-command DOM event. Register commands chat$slash_command(), accepts 0- 1-argument handlers; 1-argument handlers receive ContentSlashCommand object (ContentText subclass command user_text slots) handlers can mutate content@text passing client$stream(). echo parameter controls whether invocation recorded user message triggers loading state. Echoed commands faithfully restored bookmark/restore. (#239) Added submit_key parameter chat_ui() chat_mod_ui(): "enter" (default, Enter submits) "enter+modifier" (Ctrl/Cmd+Enter submits, plain Enter inserts line break). input remains editable response streaming — submission blocked, typing. (#251)

title: shinychat (development version)
text: input$<id>_user_input now depends allow_attachments. allow_attachments = FALSE, remains historical typed string. attachments enabled (TRUE MIME allow-list), always list ellmer Content objects (typed text, present, followed one object per attachment), separate input$<id>_user_attachments input removed. Forward either form chat method splicing !!!, e.g. chat$stream_async(!!!input$<id>_user_input). last_input reactive returned chat_mod_server() now mirrors shape input$<id>_user_input: string attachments disabled, list ellmer Content objects enabled.

title: shinychat (development version)
text: Fixed copy button code blocks working embedded contexts. (@thisisnic, #247)

title: shinychat 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-01

title: shinychat 0.4.0
text: chat UI’s rendering layer migrated Lit React. significantly improves streaming performance — incoming chunks longer clear previous DOM state — makes codebase maintainable. One trade-certain Shiny UI elements embedded chat messages may work well (e.g., inline <script> tags generally supported inside React runtime). encounter issues, please let us know.

title: shinychat 0.4.0
text: chat UI now displays model reasoning/thinking content collapsible panels assistant responses. Thinking content streams real-time animated topic labels. works providers support structured thinking (e.g., Claude’s extended thinking via ellmer) local models wrap reasoning tags. (#208) Added enable_cancel parameter chat_ui() show stop button lets users cancel -progress AI response. Press stop button hit Escape cancel. chat_mod_ui() enables cancellation default, chat_mod_server() handles cancellation wiring automatically, using stream cancellation features introduced ellmer v0.4.1. (#221) Markdown lists every item now rendered grid clickable suggestion cards. suggestion’s text content becomes card label value sent click. add short heading body text, set title attribute span — e.g. Body text shown card.. body text (title) submitted card clicked. Cards stream staggered animations support keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home/End) roving tabindex. (#219) Added chat_greeting() creating welcome messages appear chat empty. Greetings can set statically via chat_ui(greeting=) dynamically server chat_set_greeting(). automatically dismissed user sends first message. new greeting_requested input fires chat visible, empty, greeting, enabling LLM-generated welcome messages. chat_mod_server(greeting=) accepts function auto-generated greetings. (#217) Tool result cards now render images PDFs returned ellmer tools. tool returns content_image_file(), content_image_url(), content_pdf_file(), result displayed inline image PDF filename badge. Mixed content lists (e.g., list(ContentText("summary"), content_image_file("plot.png"))) rendered items interleaved order. (#225) Added footer parameter chat_ui() displaying arbitrary HTML content chat input. Useful disclaimers, attribution, interactive toolbars. Styled sensible defaults customizable via --shiny-chat-footer-font-size --shiny-chat-footer-color CSS custom properties. (#224) Tool result cards now support fullscreen toggle. Set full_screen = TRUE display list (set res$full_screen <- NA custom contents_shinychat() method) add button expands card fill viewport. Press Escape, click backdrop, use close button exit fullscreen. Added footer field ToolResultDisplay displaying custom HTML content tool result card body. (#178) chat_mod_server() now returns set_client(new_client, sync = TRUE) function swapping chat client used module runtime. sync = TRUE (default), new client inherits current conversation’s turns, system prompt, tools conversation continues seamlessly. response currently streaming, swap deferred stream completes. (#227) chat_mod_server() now returns status reactive reports current interaction state: "idle" response progress, "streaming" response actively received. (#227) chat_restore() now invisibly returns cancel function tears bookmark registrations made call. useful swapping chat client via set_client(), handles re-registration automatically. (#227)

title: shinychat 0.4.0
text: navigating links assistant messages now open new tab preserve app’s session state. Cross-origin links still show confirmation dialog; -origin links open directly. (#238)

title: shinychat 0.4.0
text: Fixed external link confirmation dialog rendering Safari. backdrop overlay appeared dialog content invisible due Bootstrap/<dialog> CSS interaction. (#201, #238) Fixed pressing Escape dismiss external link dialog leaving broken state subsequent link clicks longer worked. (#238) Fixed issue user chat messages display default assistant icon. (#162)

title: shinychat 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-11-20

title: shinychat 0.3.0
text: chat_mod_server() now returns list reactives last_input last_turn, well functions update_user_input(), append() clear() chat. (#130, #143, #145)

title: shinychat 0.3.0
text: Added chat_restore() adds Shiny bookmarking hooks save restore ellmer chat client. (#28, #82) Added update_chat_user_input() programmatically updating user input chat UI element. (#78) shinychat now shows tool call request results UI, feature enabled default chat_app() chat module (chat_mod_server()). using chat_append() chat_ui(), set stream = "content" call $stream_async() method ellmer::Chat client ensure tool calls included chat stream output. Learn tool calling UI article. (#52) Added chat_append(icon=...) chat_ui(icon_assistant=...) customizing icon appears next assistant responses. (#88)

title: shinychat 0.3.0
text: chat_app() now correctly restores chat client state refreshing app, e.g. reloading page. (#71) External links chat messages chat_ui() now open new tab default, confirmation dialog. (#120)

title: shinychat 0.3.0
text: chat input longer submits incomplete text user activated IME completions (e.g. typing Japanese Chinese). (#85)

title: shinychat 0.3.0
text: consolidated <shiny-chat-message> <shiny-user-message> components single <shiny-chat-message> component data-role attribute indicate whether ’s “assistant” “user” message. likely minimal impact apps, custom styles. update shiny-user-message rules use shiny-chat-message[data-role="user"]. (#101) chat UI’s send input button now identified class .shiny-chat-btn-send. (@DeepanshKhurana, #138)

title: shinychat 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-05-16

title: shinychat 0.2.0
text: Added new output_markdown_stream() markdown_stream() functions allow streaming markdown content client. useful showing Generative AI responses real-time Shiny app, outside chat interface. (#23) chat_ui() output_markdown_stream() now support arbitrary Shiny UI elements inside messages. allows gathering input user (e.g., selectInput()), displaying rich output (e.g., htmlwidgets like plotly), . (#29) Added new chat_clear() function clear chat messages. (#25) Added chat_app(), chat_mod_ui() chat_mod_server(). chat_app() takes ellmer chat client launches simple Shiny app interface chat. chat_mod_ui() chat_mod_server() replicate interface Shiny module, easily adding simple chat interface connected specific ellmer chat client. (#36) promise returned chat_append() now resolves content streamed chat. (#49)

title: shinychat 0.2.0
text: chat_append(), chat_append_message() chat_clear() now work Shiny modules without needing namespace id Chat component. (#37) chat_append() now logs throws silent error stream errors reason. prevents app crashing stream interrupted. can still use promises::catch() handle error app code desired. (#46)

title: shinychat 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-18 Initial CRAN submission.
